column2: # Step 1: Read the event distribution data from the CSV file
# Step 2: Convert percentage strings to numeric values for calculations
# Step 3: Gather data into long format for easier processing
pivot_longer(cols = c(production, comprehension, gap_c2p, gap_p2c, pause_p, pause_c),
# Step 4: Calculate average percentage for each event by condition and eng_level
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'condition', 'eng_level'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
# Step 5: Rename engagement levels and set the correct order
mutate(eng_level = factor(eng_level, levels = c("EC", "AL", "PL")))  # Ensure order is correct
# Step 6: Rename event types and set the correct order for x-axis
# Step 7: Create the grouped bar chart, faceted by condition
ggplot(data = average_distribution, aes(x = event_type, y = avg_percentage, fill = eng_level)) +
geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +  # Increase distance between bars
scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +  # Convert to percentage format
axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1, size = 11),  # Increase size of x-axis ticks
strip.text = element_text(size = 14),  # Increase size of facet titles (Human, Robot)
legend.position = "right",  # Position the legend on the right
legend.title = element_text(size = 10),  # Adjust the legend title size
legend.text = element_text(size = 8),  # Adjust the legend text size
legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm")  # Adjust size of legend keys
scale_fill_manual(values = colorspace::qualitative_hcl(3, palette = "Set 2")) +  # Use a suitable colorblind-friendly palette
facet_wrap(~ condition_label, scales = "free")  # Create separate plots for Human and Robot
